data_in.description: ESI_NEG_SRFA.d raw FT-ICR mass spectrometry data file
SRFA.ref calibration reference file for mass calibration

data_out.description: CSV table of assigned molecular formulas with mass error and score fields

domain_knowledge: Hanning apodization is a windowing function applied to time-domain FT-ICR transients before FFT to reduce spectral leakage; zero-filling (padding with zeros) increases frequency-domain resolution.
Mass-domain calibration uses reference compounds of known exact mass to establish a calibration curve that corrects systematic mass errors across the spectrum.
CHO elemental constraints (carbon, hydrogen, oxygen only) restrict the molecular formula search space for small organic molecules, reducing false positive assignments.
Mass error is typically expressed in parts-per-million (ppm) and represents the difference between measured m/z and theoretical m/z; smaller absolute error indicates better formula confidence.
CoreMS MSParameters encapsulation allows parametrized configuration of the entire processing pipeline including apodization, noise threshold, calibration tolerance, and formula search settings.

direct_checks: script_runs: execute CoreMS FT-ICR pipeline (apodization with Hanning window and one zero-fill, noise thresholding, MzDomainCalibration, SearchMolecularFormulas with CHO constraints) on ESI_NEG_SRFA.d without raising exceptions
file_format_is: output is a CSV or pandas-compatible table
field_present: output table contains columns for molecular formula assignments
field_present: output table contains a mass error field
field_present: output table contains a score field
row_count_equals: output table has at least one row of assigned molecular formula data (no empty result sets)
expert_review: verify that assigned molecular formulas respect CHO atom constraints (only Carbon, Hydrogen, Oxygen atoms present)
verify that mass error values are within physically plausible range for FT-ICR MS (typically < 5 ppm for high-resolution instruments)
verify that score values reflect confidence metrics appropriate to molecular formula assignment algorithms
expert judgment on whether Hanning apodization with one zero-fill is correctly applied to the FT-ICR time-domain data
expert judgment on whether MzDomainCalibration against SRFA.ref produces reasonable calibration (inspect calibration residuals if available)

cmd: import sys; from corems.data_source.factory import MorningstarMSFileFactory; from corems.mass_spectrum.calc import ChemicalFormula; import pandas as pd; import numpy as np; raw = MorningstarMSFileFactory("{inputs.raw_ftms_data}").file; raw.apply_apodization(window='hanning'); raw.zero_filling(1); raw.noise_threshold(); raw.calibrate_mass_axis(reference_file="{inputs.calibration_ref}"); spectrum = raw.spectrum; assignments = []; [assignments.append({'m_z': peak.mz, 'formula': str(peak.best_formula), 'mass_error_ppm': peak.mass_error, 'score': peak.formula_score}) for peak in spectrum if peak.best_formula and all(peak.best_formula.composition.get(e, 0) >= 0 for e in ['C', 'H', 'O'])]; df = pd.DataFrame(assignments); df.to_csv('{task_id}_formulas.csv', index=False)

inputs: ESI_NEG_SRFA.d raw FT-ICR mass spectrometry data file
SRFA.ref calibration reference file for mass calibration
methodology_summary: Load FT-ICR raw data (ESI_NEG_SRFA.d) and calibration reference (SRFA.ref) into CoreMS factory.
Apply Hanning window apodization with single zero-fill to time-domain transient.
Remove noise via intensity thresholding on frequency-domain spectrum.
Calibrate m/z axis using MzDomainCalibration against reference compounds in SRFA.ref.
Search and assign molecular formulas using CHO elemental constraints; compute mass error and formula score for each assignment.
Validation: Output CSV contains formula assignments with mass error values; all assigned formulas obey CHO elemental constraints and exhibit mass error consistent with FT-ICR instrumental resolution.

Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
workflow_description: 1. Load ESI_NEG_SRFA.d FT-ICR raw data and SRFA.ref calibration reference using CoreMS data factory. 2. Apply Hanning apodization with one zero-fill to the time-domain spectrum. 3. Apply noise thresholding to remove low-intensity peaks. 4. Perform mass-domain calibration using MzDomainCalibration against SRFA.ref reference data. 5. Execute SearchMolecularFormulas with elemental constraints limited to C, H, O atoms. 6. Export assigned molecular formulas with calculated mass error and formula score metrics to CSV format.
